assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 29-31: '## Deployment Context' section states 'The Source Repositories table does not include a Deployment Context column. Per backward-compatibility rules, deployment context defaults to `upstream` for internal tracking. Coordination Guidance is omitted from remediation task descriptions.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: all four task descriptions have Implementation Notes sections without any Coordination Guidance subsection. Task 1 Implementation Notes (lines 44-53), Task 2 Implementation Notes (lines 100-105), Task 3 Implementation Notes (lines 165-170), Task 4 Implementation Notes (lines 228-233) -- none contain a 'Coordination Guidance' heading or content. Confirmed by absence of the string 'Coordination Guidance' anywhere in the 261-line file.

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md completes full data extraction including CVE data, stream scope resolution, ecosystem detection, version impact table, affects versions correction, and cross-stream impact analysis. remediation.md produces all four remediation tasks (Case A: 2 tasks for 2.2.x, Case B: 2 preemptive tasks for 2.1.x) and post-triage actions. No error messages or warnings about missing data in either file.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 31: 'Per backward-compatibility rules, deployment context defaults to `upstream` for internal tracking.' The triage produces standard outputs: version impact table with correct affected/unaffected classifications, affects versions correction (RHTPA 2.0.0 -> 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2), Case A remediation tasks for 2.2.x stream, Case B preemptive tasks for 2.1.x stream with security-preemptive labels and Related links. All standard triage behavior is preserved.
